query: Glimpse: Fast, local-first, Open-Source dictation built for you review

results.title: TextJam: The multi-player AI editor, for everyone who writes.
results.content:
Pete DeLaurentis
TextJam

TextJam

@zidream Yes indeed, it is a guitar pick! With lines of text :) We want our logo to evoke "jam session", and a spirit of fearless creative experimentation.

To answer your question – TextJam currently works well on iPad sizes 11-13 inches. If you have an iPad with a nice keyboard, it is a fantastic experience. We've found the onscreen keyboard can work well in a pinch too, especially when used with TextJam's dictation features.  
  
We're working on also bringing a great experience to smaller devices, including smaller tablets and mobile phones. If you have a chance to try it on your iPad, we'd love to hear about your experience.

Ran Luo

Nice! A little expensive for me, but it can be really useful. Bug caught - the delete account function did not work.

Pete DeLaurentis
TextJam

TextJam

@alexey\_sokoloff Thanks for flagging Alexey – the delete function has been fixed. We'll reach out via email to make sure it works for you.

Nitesh Padghan

Just played with TextJam, feels like the first AI writing tool that actually gets collaboration. The pen/pencil thing is weirdly intuitive, and resizing text with gestures is a wild touch.

@Rano\_9, the resolution drop in free versions is usually where they force you to pay. I'm trying to change that with my first launch, WebAITool. We offer high-quality output for free because I believe basic AI utilities should be accessible to everyone. Check it out and let me know if the quality holds up for your batch edits!

Might be a stress-free option for your off-peak months!"

been using it for years. it's free, it's efficient, it works well, it's easy to fix mistakes, and the developers really seem to care about making it a good product rather than making money off of it. no ads, no hidden feens, literally just a good product that's easy to use. to the devs, thank you so much. you have made my life so much easier with school and creative projects. y'all deserve all the best.

results.title: Trace: Workflow Automations for the Human AI Workforce
results.content:
Artur
Trace

Trace

@timcha\_cherkasov  @kay\_arkain Thanks a lot! That unified context layer is exactly what we believe unlocks the next step - an agentic infrastructure that learns from your company and introduces automation without human intervention. A long way to go but you can get the glimpse of it with the current version!

Andrei

@timcha\_cherkasov  @kay\_arkain  @arthur\_romanov Not that long to go, imho 😂

Vlad
Trace

Trace

@timcha\_cherkasov  @kay\_arkain Really appreciate that! We’ve seen how scattered tools make AI feel "broken", even though we know that the tool is available and it should work. Proofs that bringing everything into one context is what makes it click and finally start working.

Andrei [...] #### What needs improvement

Clear value once you’re using it, but onboarding could get you there faster with more opinionated starter workflows. Better visibility into what’s automated vs handled by humans can also help as workflows grow in complexity.   
Also, I think, something like ROI view (time saved, steps automated etc) would make internal adoption even easier - considering sometimes you have to "sell" this to your colleagues.

#### vs Alternatives

We haven't compared Trace to other solutions, just to what we could built ourselves. Obviously we decided to outsource this (i.e. use Trace) and focus on our product instead.

Kate Ramakaieva
FuseBase

FuseBase

#### What's great [...] weve built 100+ automation triggers and actions that work exactly this way. agents do the work, humans approve the output. it scales without losing control.

the teams building for this hybrid model are going to win the next wave.

John Xie
Taskade AI Mobile: Supercharge Workflows

Taskade AI Mobile: Supercharge Workflows

the human-in-the-loop workflow angle is underexplored. most automation tools assume everything should be fully automated. but the highest-value workflows are hybrid: AI handles the repetitive 80%, humans handle the judgment calls.

the teams building for this middle ground are going to win the next wave. fully manual is too slow. fully automated is too risky for anything important.
